description: Phase 5 — Conflict register. Tensions that arise when multiple standards/objectives are applied to one organization. Each conflict is a graph edge of type 'TENSION_WITH'. `severity`: low | medium | high. `resolution` is the recommended reconciliation pattern the Nirva engine surfaces.

conflicts.cause: Heavyweight documentation & change control vs. responding-to-change preference.
conflicts.risk: Either bureaucratic drag or undocumented, non-auditable changes.
conflicts.resolution: Define 'just enough' documented information; treat the product backlog, definition-of-done and CI/CD logs as the controlled records. ISO 9001:2015 is outcome-based, not document-count-based — exploit that.

conflicts.cause: Purpose limitation & minimization vs. desire to collect maximal behavioral data.
conflicts.resolution: Privacy-by-design: aggregate/anonymize, consent-gated event tiers, on-device computation, documented lawful basis per purpose, retention limits.

conflicts.cause: Friction of MFA, session limits, least-privilege vs. frictionless UX.
conflicts.risk: Shadow IT, control bypass, or insecure convenience defaults.
conflicts.resolution: Risk-based / adaptive authentication, passkeys, SSO, progressive security tied to data sensitivity. Measure both control efficacy and task completion.

conflicts.cause: Approval workflows & segregation of duties vs. experimentation velocity.
conflicts.risk: Either shipped non-compliant features or stalled roadmap.
conflicts.resolution: Compliance-as-code: policy checks in CI, pre-approved control patterns ('paved roads'), sandbox tiers with graduated controls by exposure.

conflicts.cause: Opt-in consent & soft-opt-in limits vs. broad outreach goals.
conflicts.risk: Unlawful marketing, consent-record gaps, regulator action.
conflicts.resolution: Consent & preference center as system-of-record; honor opt-outs across channels; lawful-basis tagging on every audience segment.

conflicts.cause: Legal duty to keep records vs. data-subject right to be forgotten.
conflicts.risk: Either statutory breach (premature deletion) or privacy breach (over-retention).
conflicts.resolution: Retention schedule mapped to legal basis; erasure honored except where overriding legal obligation applies (document the exemption); pseudonymize where deletion is blocked.

conflicts.cause: Disclosure of training data/logic vs. protecting proprietary models.
conflicts.risk: Either IP leakage or non-compliant opacity.
conflicts.resolution: Tiered transparency: public model cards & intended-use statements; confidential technical documentation for regulators under NDA; explainability surfaces without exposing weights.

conflicts.risk: Change-induced outages or change freezes that block delivery.
conflicts.resolution: Progressive delivery (canary/blue-green), error budgets (SRE), automated rollback, change risk scoring tied to BIA-critical services.

conflicts.cause: Decarbonization capex vs. quarterly financial performance.
conflicts.risk: Greenwashing or missed targets and disclosure liability.
conflicts.resolution: Internal carbon price, transition plan with phased capex, link exec comp to validated targets, double-materiality prioritization.

conflicts.cause: In-country data residency mandates vs. centralized 'one data' architecture.
conflicts.risk: Illegal cross-border transfer or fragmented, costly architecture.
conflicts.resolution: Regional data planes with a global control plane; SCCs/adequacy mechanisms; metadata-only central graph, data stays in region.

conflicts.cause: Fully automated decisions vs. mandated meaningful human oversight.
conflicts.risk: Prohibited automated decision-making, GDPR Art.22 breach.
conflicts.resolution: Human-in-the-loop for high-risk decisions, override mechanisms, decision logging, defined escalation thresholds.

conflicts.cause: API openness & transparency vs. attack-surface minimization.
conflicts.resolution: Zero-trust API gateways, scoped tokens, rate limiting, published-but-authenticated APIs, security review in API lifecycle.
